danger_signals.visual_form: Animated sprite entities with distinct creature designs
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats that must be defeated; collision causes damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Explosive animation spreading in cardinal directions from bomb origin
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate hazard to player and all entities; indiscriminate damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber as larger, specialized character sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring strategic engagement; stage-blocking encounters

danger_signals.visual_form: Destructible wall tiles distinct from permanent walls
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards that hide enemies and block progression until cleared

danger_signals.visual_form: Time hole visual effect transporting characters across eras
danger_signals.meaning: World state change; narrative danger forcing new context and challenges

safety_signals.visual_form: Level completion and world unlock state progression
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression milestone; access to replay and password system

safety_signals.visual_form: Ally Bombermen trapped in visible cages on stages
safety_signals.meaning: Rescuable entities that assist by destroying soft blocks; aid signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items appearing after soft block destruction
safety_signals.meaning: Enhancement opportunities: increased bomb range, punch/kick ability, throw mechanic

safety_signals.visual_form: Defeated enemies become collectable eggs with two type variants (organic/mechanical)
safety_signals.meaning: Rideable allies providing tactical advantage and stage navigation assistance

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden stage portals accessible via two-player Power Glove mechanic
safety_signals.meaning: Bonus stage access; reward for cooperative play and exploration

faction_indicators.color_identity: White and Black character color coding
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Classic Bomberman silhouette with bomb signature
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Four Bomber Kings visually distinct from standard Bomberman with unique sprites (Bazooka Bomber, Jet Bomber, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Time-displaced across five worlds (prehistoric to final era); protagonist positioning in narrative cutscenes

faction_indicators.faction: Great Bomber and Four Bomber Kings (antagonists/rivals)
faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct character color palettes for each king variant
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unique character designs with specialized visual identities (jetpack, bazooka attachment visuals)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each bomber king has reduced stats (speed, bomb count, range) for 10 seconds after ability use; visual feedback of power depletion
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear as boss encounters in level 7 of each world; confrontation stages with dedicated arena layouts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through four lights collision event and time manipulation mechanic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Brain entity escaped from flying saucer explosion; commands Four Bomber Kings remotely
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence communicated through temporal anomaly (time hole); influences stage design across five time periods

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White and Black Bomber returning home on space shuttle; calm space environment
Four lights approach and collide with shuttle; temporal vortex visual effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced transport to prehistoric age; characters displaced across time eras

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage theming with primitive environmental design; ancient creature enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: First encounter with Great Bomber and Four Bomber Kings; introduction of core threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive era progression through five distinct world themes; visual stage differentiation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating boss encounters; repetition of Four Bomber Kings with increasing difficulty; world-specific enemy types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage completion screen; world unlock state; password generation for replay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to next world; ability to replay stages; progression checkpoint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena stage selection; multiple Bomberman characters in selectable states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free-for-all, team, or champion round engagement; CPU difficulty indicators; customization menu access (Maniac Mode)

environmental_storytelling.example: Five sequential worlds represent different historical eras players must traverse; visual stage design communicates temporal displacement (prehistoric > final era)
Distinct creature rosters for each era; organic vs. mechanical type classification signals world technological/biological context
Rescued Bombermen act as soft-block destroyers; their presence and behavior communicate collaborative survival theme in hostile environment
Four Bomber Kings appear in world level 7 (pre-final); world boss appears in level 8; spatial progression and encounter order communicate escalating threat hierarchy
Items hidden in soft blocks; collection signals area safety and resource availability; reinforces destruction-as-progression mechanic
Each Bomberman variant (Bazooka Bomber, Jet Bomber) has unique sprite modifications; ability cooldown (10-second stat reduction) communicated through gameplay feedback

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrolling stage, moving toward player collision; defeat converts to collectible egg
Transform into egg form; become rideable if collected (organic or mechanical type restriction applies)
Move autonomously through stage destroying soft blocks; assist progression

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appear as level 7 and final boss encounters; distinct sprite and movement patterns per variant

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: 10-second stat reduction (speed, bomb count, range decreased); visual gameplay feedback of vulnerability window
Standard movement, bomb placement, collision with hazards causes defeat
One player can toss other player to warp portals; enables secret stage access in two-player mode
Face Four Bomber Kings and Great Bomber across three rounds; defeat triggers credits roll

notes: Super Bomberman 4 communicates world-state primarily through narrative cutscenes (time hole collision event) and stage theming across five sequential eras. Faction identity relies on character sprite differentiation rather than color-coded teams; antagonist faction (Four Bomber Kings, Great Bomber) is communicated through boss encounter design and repeated confrontations. Danger signals center on collision mechanics (bombs, enemies) and environmental hazards (soft blocks). Safety signals emerge through power-up collection, ally rescue, and creature egg mechanics. The game lacks explicit political faction systems but establishes temporal conflict through revenge narrative (Bagular's will driving the Four Bomber Kings). Behavioral tells primarily indicate entity state transitions (alive-to-egg conversion, caged-to-free allies) rather than emotional or ideological positioning.
